commands.repair_reason: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the patch is focused and no actionable defects were found; the remaining action is maintainer review and merge gating.; Cleared: The diff only changes heartbeat runner logic and a focused Vitest file, with no dependency, workflow, credential, package, or code-execution surface change. (sha=ef2952c51747eb50535ae4359021393c602fb29a)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds active embedded-run checks to heartbeat dispatch and tests that scheduled heartbeats return `requests-in-flight` instead of entering the reply path while same-agent embedded work is active.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +31. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection and after-fix proof: current main lacks the embedded-run pre-dispatch check while the downstream queue drops heartbeat turns during active runs. The PR body shows the scheduled wake returning `requests-in-flight` and then dispatching after the active embedded run clears.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the patch is focused and no actionable defects were found; the remaining action is maintainer review and merge gating.

target.title: fix #85871: [Bug]: Heartbeat scheduler silently fails to fire on 5.20 and all 5.x versions (regression from 4.23)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: scheduled heartbeat turns could reach the reply path while the same agent had active embedded work, where heartbeat turns are intentionally dropped.
- Why it matters / User impact: affected 5.x installs can look like heartbeat is configured, but after gateway startup the scheduled heartbeat can be consumed without visible heartbeat activity, leaving users with no `[heartbeat] started` / dispatch signal to diagnose.
- Root cause: `runHeartbeatOnce` checked active reply runs and queued session lanes before calling `getReplyFromConfig`, but it did not check active embedded-agent runs for the same agent. When a scheduled heartbeat reached the reply layer while an embedded main-session run was active, the reply queue policy dropped heartbeat turns to avoid queueing heartbeat followups. That dropped reply surfaced to the heartbeat runner as an empty result, so the tick could be recorded as handled without ever delivering the heartbeat prompt to the main agent session.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the patch moves the deferral to the heartbeat runner boundary, before reply dispatch. It reuses the active embedded-run session-key registry and applies the same same-agent background-work invariant already used for active reply runs. That preserves the scheduler/wake retry contract: scheduled heartbeats now produce retryable `requests-in-flight` while embedded work is active, instead of relying on the downstream reply queue to handle a heartbeat it intentionally drops.
- Fix classification: Root cause fix.
- Solution: defer scheduled heartbeat dispatch earlier in `runHeartbeatOnce` when any active embedded run belongs to the target agent.
- What changed: heartbeat runner now checks active embedded-run session keys alongside active reply runs and returns the retryable `requests-in-flight` result before calling `getReplyFromConfig`.
- What did NOT change: manual/immediate heartbeat wake semantics, reply queue policy, outbound delivery, heartbeat cadence calculation, public config shape, and persisted session formats are unchanged.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for the live gateway scheduler firing and reaching dispatch on a shortened interval, and high for the active embedded-run deferral/retry boundary. Medium for the reporter's exact Ubuntu/Ollama deployment because this proof used a local configured provider/model and a 2-second interval instead of the full 5-minute production cadence.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: the heartbeat runner is the source-of-truth dispatch boundary that decides whether scheduled heartbeat work should call the reply/model path. The active embedded-run registry is the owner for same-agent embedded work already in flight. This patch reads that owner registry before dispatch; it does not add downstream string matching, queue special cases, mirrored state, or persistence projections.
- Patch quality notes: This intentionally changes retry behavior at the dispatch boundary. The retry uses the existing heartbeat wake path for `requests-in-flight`; it avoids queueing heartbeat followups and avoids marking due heartbeat work as completed before a model turn actually runs.

## Linked context

- Fixes #85871.
- Existing PR: #88970.
- Current review finding addressed: RF-001 from ClawSweeper asked for proof beyond a local scheduled runner/wake boundary, specifically a real gateway scheduler interval with heartbeat startup/dispatch evidence.
- Related open PR / same-contract scan: current `origin/main` was synced before verification and did not already cover this active-embedded-run heartbeat deferral. This update remains a focused two-file heartbeat-runner change and does not broaden provider, channel, or config contracts.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: The live gateway heartbeat scheduler now fires on its configured interval and reaches agent/model dispatch, and scheduled heartbeats defer instead of being silently consumed while same-agent embedded work is active.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway started through `pnpm gateway:dev` with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, isolated `openclaw.json`, a short `every: 2s` heartbeat, `target: none`, and a real configured provider/model. The busy/retry boundary was also exercised through the actual scheduler wake path with active embedded work registered for `agent:main:main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `daily-fix run-validation --name live-heartbeat-gateway -- bash heartbeat-live-gateway-proof.sh`, then `daily-fix run-validation --name heartbeat-busy-retry -- tsx heartbeat-scheduled-embedded-proof.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: Live gateway log excerpt shows the gateway became ready, the scheduler emitted `[heartbeat] started`, and the same heartbeat turn reached agent dispatch:

  ```text
  READY=ok
  HEARTBEAT_STARTED=1
  HEARTBEAT_DISPATCH_AFTER_STARTED=1
  2026-06-09T01:02:41.432+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (...; 18.4s)
  2026-06-09T01:02:41.438+08:00 [gateway] ready
  2026-06-09T01:02:42.052+08:00 [heartbeat] started
  2026-06-09T01:02:56.565+08:00 [agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] ... phase=core-plugin-tools ... model-provider-policy:1ms ... attempt:tools-allow:0ms
  ```

  The busy/retry proof then showed the first scheduled wake deferred before dispatch while active embedded work existed, and the retry dispatched after the busy run cleared:

  ```text
  [proof] active embedded run registered: sessionId=proof-active-embedded-run sessionKey=agent:main:main
  [heartbeat] started
  [proof] scheduled wake attempt #1: source=interval intent=scheduled reason=interval activeBefore=["agent:main:main"] result={"status":"skipped","reason":"requests-in-flight"} dispatchCount=0
  [proof] active embedded run cleared after first busy skip: sessionId=proof-active-embedded-run
  [proof] model dispatch reached after busy cleared: dispatchCount=1
  [proof] scheduled wake attempt #2: source=interval intent=scheduled reason=interval activeBefore=[] result={"status":"ran","durationMs":153} dispatchCount=1
  [proof] PASS: scheduled heartbeat deferred while active embedded work existed, then wake retry dispatched after it cleared
  ```
- Observed result after fix: A real gateway heartbeat cycle no longer sat silent after startup; it logged scheduler startup and reached agent/model dispatch on the configured interval. When same-agent embedded work was active, the scheduled wake returned the retryable `requests-in-flight` result before model dispatch, and the retry dispatched once that embedded work cleared.
- What was not tested: The reporter's exact Ubuntu/Ollama install and the full 5-minute interval / 10+ minute wall-clock wait. The local proof shortened the interval to 2 seconds to exercise the same live gateway scheduler path without waiting multiple production cycles.

## Tests and validation

- Target test file: `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts` covers active embedded work on the resolved heartbeat session and another same-agent session. Existing scheduler/wake, outbound target, gateway runtime-service, and reply queue tests were also run.
- Scenario locked in: scheduled heartbeat dispatch returns `requests-in-flight` before model dispatch when active embedded work exists for the same agent.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the heartbeat runner boundary where the bug occurs, without relying on the downstream reply queue drop behavior or an external model/channel.
- `daily-fix run-validation --name live-heartbeat-gateway -- bash heartbeat-live-gateway-proof.sh` — passed; real `pnpm gateway:dev` startup with isolated config logged `READY=ok`, `[heartbeat] started`, and `HEARTBEAT_DISPATCH_AFTER_STARTED=1` on the shortened heartbeat interval.
- `daily-fix run-validation --name heartbeat-busy-retry -- tsx heartbeat-scheduled-embedded-proof.ts` — passed; real scheduled `source=interval` / `intent=scheduled` heartbeat tick returned `requests-in-flight` while `agent:main:main` had active embedded work, then the wake retry reached model dispatch and returned `ran` after the embedded run cleared.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.skips-busy-session-lane.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-wake.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue-policy.test.ts` — passed; targeted heartbeat, scheduler/wake, outbound target, gateway runtime-service, and reply queue regressions completed with unit-fast, gateway, and infra shards green.

## Risk checklist

- Risk labels considered: compatibility, session-state.
- Risk explanation: merge risk is limited to scheduled heartbeat retry behavior when the same agent already has active embedded work. In that case the runner now returns the existing retryable `requests-in-flight` status instead of continuing into a downstream reply path that can drop heartbeat turns.
- Why acceptable: this aligns scheduled heartbeat handling with the existing active-reply-run guard and existing wake retry contract, so the behavior becomes retriable at the owner boundary instead of silently consumed downstream. Manual and immediate heartbeats keep their existing behavior.
- Public contract / config boundary: `openclaw.json` appears only in the local proof setup; the patch does not change public config schema, defaults, migration behavior, provider selection, channel delivery contracts, or persisted session data format.
- Out of scope / not changed: no provider-specific Ollama workaround, no full 5-minute production wait in local proof, no channel delivery rewrite, and no reply queue policy rewrite.

## Current review state

- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
  - RF-001 `[P1]` from ClawSweeper: fixed/proven on current head. The updated proof now starts the real gateway, waits for a shortened scheduler interval, observes `[heartbeat] started`, observes dispatch after startup, and separately proves the busy defer/retry dispatch boundary with active embedded work.
- Remaining maintainer decision points:
  - The proof uses a shortened 2-second heartbeat interval rather than waiting 10+ minutes on the reporter's exact Ubuntu/Ollama setup.
  - The local provider/model differs from the reporter's Ollama deployment, but it exercises the same gateway scheduler and heartbeat dispatch path.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
